name: Autophagy Induction Coverage Repair

scope.question: Which source-completeness gaps for Autophagy Induction need repair before ordinary surveillance resumes, and do active/no-results registries or recent reviews change the public read?

summary: Coverage repair staged source, study, finding, and outlook records for the high-priority Autophagy Induction gaps identified after June 11 surveillance. The repair adds completed no-results KH-1 autophagy registry context, recruiting spermidine MCI sleep/autophagy context, active-not-recruiting spermidine elderly coronary artery disease context, stale unknown-status fasting-mimicking diet immunosenescence/frailty context, recruiting trehalose autophagic-flux context, and a direct 2026 polyamine/autophagy muscle-aging review boundary. The staged outlook keeps evidence_stage human_biomarker_signal, momentum mixed, and confidence low.

materiality_decision.rationale: The repair materially improves registry normalization, review triangulation, and taxonomy-boundary coverage, but it does not add posted results that would change stage, momentum, confidence, or the core interpretation.

search_log.query: NCT07383311, NCT06186102, NCT07255300, NCT07354620, NCT06445569, NCT04928963, and NCT05593549 direct registry checks
search_log.summary: Confirmed current registry status, dates, endpoint scope, enrollment, and no-results status for the flagged Autophagy Induction coverage gaps. NCT06445569 is completed with no posted results; NCT07383311 and NCT05593549 are recruiting with no posted results; NCT06186102 and NCT07354620 are active not recruiting with no posted results; NCT04928963 is unknown-status and stale with no posted results; NCT07255300 is recruiting with no posted results.

Confirmed PMID 42086115 as the direct 2026 Mechanisms of Ageing and Development review on polyamines, spermidine, exercise, SMOX, autophagy, skeletal muscle regeneration, and aging. PMID 42169620 was screened as vaccine/microbiota/mTOR context, and PMID 42225652 as broad aging-therapeutics review context; neither added a new primary Autophagy Induction result.

search_log.source_name: Local public records and coverage assessment
search_log.query: Autophagy Induction outlook, source, study, finding, candidate-bundle, and June 11 surveillance coverage artifacts
search_log.summary: Compared the current public outlook and surveillance assessment against staged candidates. The repair prioritized registries and the direct polyamine/autophagy review that improve coverage without overloading the public map with single-arm, rapamycin/FMD combination, or broad review records.

trial_watch_checks.summary: Staged as completed no-results trial-watch context because the trial directly names autophagy stimulation and includes autophagy endpoints.
Staged as recruiting no-results trial-watch context because it directly tests spermidine and includes polyamine and eIF5A hypusination endpoints.
Staged as active no-results trial-watch context because the large older-patient spermidine phase 2 trial includes autophagy, polyamine, physical-performance, cognition, and cardiovascular endpoints.
Staged as stale unknown-status no-results trial-watch context because it is an elderly FMD immunosenescence, flu-vaccine response, and frailty-prevention registry at the autophagy/immune boundary.
Staged as recruiting no-results mechanistic context because trehalose is tested with autophagic-flux protein expression and vascular endpoints in type 2 diabetes and healthy-aging context.
Excluded from staging because it is a single-group FMD follow-up focused on body fat and risk-factor maintenance rather than interpretable autophagy induction.

Excluded from staging because the rapamycin and ProLon reverse-aging pilot is primarily nutrient-sensing, mTOR, combination, and methylation-age context rather than clean Autophagy Induction evidence.

excluded_sources.title: Follow up Study of Varapodio Trial: Effect of Longevity and Fasting Mimicking Diet on Risk Factors Age Correlated and Biomarkers of Aging
excluded_sources.reason: Recruiting no-results single-group FMD follow-up with body-fat and risk-factor maintenance endpoints. It is useful FMD surveillance context but not direct enough to stage as Autophagy Induction evidence.

excluded_sources.title: A Pilot Study on Reverse Aging (The REVERSE Study)
excluded_sources.reason: Active-not-recruiting no-results rapamycin plus ProLon methylation-age pilot. It primarily belongs to nutrient-sensing, mTOR modulation, combination-intervention, and biological-age-marker boundary coverage.

excluded_sources.title: Immunosenescence and Vaccine Efficacy in Aging: Dynamic Interplay of Gut Microbiota and mTOR Signaling Pathways
excluded_sources.reason: Review-level vaccine, microbiota, and mTOR context with autophagy mentioned through mTOR suppression. It does not add a new primary autophagy-induction result beyond the staged spermidine vaccine pilot.

excluded_sources.title: Insights into the therapeutic strategies for aging and aging-associated diseases
excluded_sources.reason: Broad aging-therapeutics review that mentions metabolic interventions and autophagy. It is too broad to stage as a public Autophagy Induction evidence record.

notes: The staged repair intentionally keeps the public evidence stage at human_biomarker_signal.
No-results registry entries are represented as regulatory/program-update findings with inconclusive direction.
Month-only ClinicalTrials.gov completion dates were normalized to the first day of the month in structured date fields and preserved as month-level timing in text notes.
KH-1 was staged without creating a new intervention record because the repair goal is source-completeness and the product is not otherwise normalized in the intervention map.
next_actions: Run source-fidelity, interpretation/forecast, taxonomy-mapping, and safety-limitations review for the staged repair bundle.

If approved and published, return Autophagy Induction to ordinary surveillance with watch items for KH-1 results, active spermidine registries, FMD unknown-status updates, and trehalose autophagic-flux results.
